commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Manual maintainer review is required because the PR is draft/protected and changes compatibility, auth identity, protocol, and node exec-approval security boundaries rather than needing a narrow automated repair.; Cleared: No concrete supply-chain or secret-handling regression was found; the auth and exec-approval security-boundary changes are intentional but require maintainer approval before merge. commands.review_summary:
The PR hardens Windows node pairing, device-token reuse, direct loopback gateway CLI auth, node exec-approval RPCs, stable node identity routing, and pending node-list presentation.

PR surface: Source +838, Tests +2404. Total +3242 across 47 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main registers node sessions by device id and only accepts file-shaped node exec approval set payloads, while the PR body supplies live Windows steps and proof for pairing and native exec approvals on the branch head. I did not run a fresh Windows current-main failure locally in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual maintainer review is required because the PR is draft/protected and changes compatibility, auth identity, protocol, and node exec-approval security boundaries rather than needing a narrow automated repair.

target.title: fix(cli): harden Windows node pairing and approvals
target.body:
## Summary
- preserve existing untagged device tokens for same-key metadata refresh approvals when the token was already valid before approval
- keep rotating for scope expansion, public-key repair, stale baseline tokens, revoked/issuer-tagged tokens, incompatible role/scopes, and malformed persisted token entries
- make devices/nodes/gateway CLI RPC helpers use the direct local gateway-client admin path for explicit loopback token/password auth, avoiding stale paired CLI identity/scope traps
- make `approvals get --node` tolerate host-native Windows exec policy payloads instead of crashing on the local approvals-file shape
- harden stable Windows node identity/event routing, node approval RPC authz, explicit loopback shared-token probing, and pending node-list presentation

## Real Behavior Proof
Behavior addressed: Windows node pairing/reconnect churn, stale paired CLI identity on explicit loopback gateway auth, native Windows exec approval payload parsing, node approval authorization, shared-token/device-token auth boundaries, and confusing commandless paired rows during node command-upgrade pairing.

Current PR head: `aec31cb62ba9e69314ab5a7b84d4049a38a10eea`.

Proof run on current head:
- GitHub PR checks: green, including `tui-pty`, `Real behavior proof`, workflow sanity, and changed-path gates.
- Local focused proof: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/program.nodes-basic.e2e.test.ts src/gateway/node-catalog.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/exec-approvals.test.ts src/gateway/node-command-policy.test.ts` passed 38 tests.
- Final changed gate: `GOMAXPROCS=2 CI=true OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 pnpm check:changed` passed on final head.
- Autoreview: `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` clean, no accepted/actionable findings.
- Azure pond gateway refreshed to `OpenClaw 2026.5.30 (aec31cb)` and restarted successfully.
- Azure pond had two signed Windows `v0.6.0-alpha.14` nodes connected, both reporting 20 commands and 7 caps; `nodes list --json` showed `pending=0`, `paired=2`, `commandlessPaired=0`.
- Signed prerelease asset used: `OpenClawCompanion-Setup-x64.exe`, release `v0.6.0-alpha.14`, published prerelease, asset digest `sha256:36405bd72e2c11bae0aa051b551b339982a66ffb59820d977ad9983dbf0b4d98`.
- Clean Windows install proof: installer and installed EXE Authenticode signatures were valid for OpenClaw Foundation; installed app reported `0.6.0-alpha.14` from `device.info`.
- Pairing proof: clean Windows VM required operator device approval, then node command-upgrade approval; after approval it converged to paired/connected with full command/capability surface and no duplicate commandless row on current CLI.
- Exec approval proof on current head: `approvals get --node --json` returned host-native policy with `enabled=true`, `defaultAction=deny`; direct `node.invoke` for `system.execApprovals.*` was rejected with the intended gateway error; `system.run` allowed `cmd.exe /c echo openclaw-clean`; `system.run` denied `cmd.exe /c echo openclaw-denied` with default-deny policy.
- Windows node feature smoke on current head: `system.which`, `device.info`, `device.status`, `camera.list`, and `screen.snapshot` succeeded on the clean Windows node. `screen.snapshot` returned a 640x480 PNG payload; `camera.list` returned zero cameras, expected for this VM.

## Validation
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ...`: 25 files / 542 tests passed earlier in the branch proof stack
- final focused CLI/gateway smoke: 4 files / 38 tests passed
- final `pnpm check:changed`: passed
- autoreview: clean, no accepted/actionable findings
- GitHub checks on pushed head: green
- Azure pond live proof: Linux gateway + two Windows nodes, signed prerelease install, pairing, exec approvals, and safe feature smoke verified

## Notes
Still draft so Vincent can review before this becomes merge-ready.
target.author: vincentkoc
